free_text: Cross sections of spallation residues produced in 1*A
GeV Pb-208 on proton reactions.
.University of Santiago de Compostela,Spain.

details of
experiments.
The proton target was composed of 87.3 Mg/cm**2 liquid
hydrogen enclosed between thin titanium foils of a
total thickness of 36 mg/cm**2
.Intranuclear cascade and evaporation fission
model.
Heavy residues produced in the target were all
strongly forward focused due to the inverse reaction
kinematics.They were identified using the fragment
separator (FRS).
Two position-sensitive plastic scintillators.
The elements below terbium were identified from
an energy loss measurement in an ionisation chamber.
the heavy-ion synchrotron.
- By authors - the production cross sections are
calculated by applying several corrections-
the measured dead-time of the data-acquisition
system, the efficiency of the detection system, the
loss of fragments due to the fragment-separator
transmission, the loss of fragments due to incompletely
stripped ions, the influence of beam attenuation and
secondary reactions in the liquid-hydrogen target and
in the other  layers  of  matter  inside  the  fragment
separator.
.All uncertainties of the used corrections lead
to a final systematic error of 9% to 23% for z from 82
to 61.
.The data were received from S.Leray.
